business.headline: RBI auctions Rs 32,000 crore in govt bonds: Are they among the safest investments?Government bonds are back in the spotlight after the RBI's latest Rs 32,000 crore auction. What makes them different from other investments, and who are they best suited for? Have a look.

8th Pay Commission: Why its impact goes far beyond government salariesEconomists say the 8th Pay Commission will affect far more than central government salaries and pensions. Its recommendations could shape consumer demand, state finances, inflation and fiscal planning.

File Early, Stay Relaxed: Why the Income Tax Department is sending SMS alertsThousands of taxpayers are receiving SMS reminders from the Income Tax Department asking them to file their ITR early. What's behind these messages?

Sensex ends 828 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; RIL up 2%The BSE Sensex surged 827.57 points, or 1.08%, to close at 77,569.39, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 244.10 points, or 1.02%, to settle at 24,206.90.

Why wait for the IPO? Some investors are buying shares much earlier. You can tooLong before an IPO opens, and sometimes even years before a company files its draft papers with the market regulator, its shares can already be bought and sold legally in what's known as the unlisted or pre-IPO market.

Income tax department launches ITR-7 Excel utility for AY 2026-27With the utility now live, eligible taxpayers can download it, fill in the required information offline, validate the details, generate a JSON file and upload it on the Income Tax e-Filing portal to complete the return filing process.

TCS's hiring surprise: Why India's biggest IT firm is adding jobs againTCS added 9,279 employees in the April-June quarter after steep headcount declines in late 2025. The shift reflects selective hiring, redeployment and rising demand for AI and digital skills.

Govt eyes stronger forex reserves; FM likely to meet banks soonThe government's move comes at a time when India's foreign exchange reserves have declined sharply. The reserves fell from around $728 billion in February to nearly $682 billion by the end of May.

Home loan on a rented house? Here's what you can still claim under new tax regimeAs taxpayers file their ITRs, understanding home loan tax rules has become more important than ever. In this edition of 'KNOW YOUR TAX', we explain what you can still claim on a rented property under the new tax regime.

TCS, Infosys, HCLTech rise up to 4%: Why are IT stocks rising today?TCS's stronger-than-expected June-quarter results pushed IT stocks higher on Friday. The Nifty IT index climbed 1.82%, while the BSE Focused IT index gained nearly 2%.

Sensex jumps 800 points: Why is stock market rising today?Around 10 am, the BSE Sensex was trading 800.61 points, or 1.04%, higher at 77,542.43, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 247.80 points, or 1.03%, to 24,210.60.

Sensex, Nifty open in green lifted by IT stocks as TCS earnings cheer marketsThe BSE Sensex opened at 77,395.63 and climbed 676.12 points, or 0.88%, to 77,417.94 in early trade. The NSE Nifty50 rose 196.30 points, or 0.82%, to 24,159.10. The benchmarks had also ended higher in the previous session after recovering from Wednesday's sharp sell-off.

Why TCS may have to rethink its dividend to win the AI raceTCS is winning more AI work, but analysts say scaling it will need heavier investment. That could push the company to rebalance dividends and future growth spending.

EPF Scheme 2026: What it is, who benefits and what it means for youFrom simpler withdrawal rules to new safeguards for retirement savings, the EPF Scheme, 2026 brings several important changes. Here's a quick look at who stands to benefit and what the new rules mean for you.

Tax regime to e-verify: Income Tax Department's 7 golden rules for ITR filingThe Income Tax Department has issued seven golden rules for filing ITRs for AY 2026-27. The checklist urges early preparation, careful verification and timely e-verification to avoid delays and lost benefits.

TCS adds over 9,200 employees in Q1, logs biggest hiring in over a yearAlongside higher hiring, TCS reported a solid financial performance for the June quarter. Consolidated net profit stood at Rs 13,349 crore, while revenue rose 14% year-on-year to Rs 72,275 crore.

TCS Q1 results: Revenue jumps 14%; IT major declares Rs 12 interim dividendThe company posted double-digit growth in both revenue and profit, helped by steady demand from clients, especially in the banking and financial services sector. It also announced an interim dividend of Rs 12 per share for its shareholders.

Sensex, Nifty close higher despite US-Iran tensions; TCS results in focusBenchmark indices closed higher on Thursday as investors returned to beaten-down stocks after Wednesday's sharp sell-off. The rebound was led by midcaps and realty shares, while Middle East tensions, crude prices and earnings remained in focus.

India's insurance industry is seeing a Gen Z takeover, but there's a twistA report says Gen Z advisors are selling more insurance policies than older peers. Experienced advisors still dominate higher-value life cover and long-term protection sales.

Aviva becomes first foreign insurer to fully own India life businessAviva has agreed to buy Dabur Invest Corp's remaining 26% stake in its India life venture. The deal makes it the first foreign insurer to fully own an Indian life business under the new FDI rules.

entertainment.headline: Vicky Donor, Badhaai Ho inspired me to do Super Subbu: Sundeep Kishan Actor Sundeep Kishan said Vicky Donor and Badhaai Ho inspired him to headline Super Subbu. He said the Telugu series uses humour and sensitivity to discuss sex education without sensationalism.

Thamizh Murugan: Dhanush rides an elephant, wields a vel in striking first look Actor Dhanush's Thamizh Murugan first look is out and marks his reunion with director Vetri Maaran for their fifth film together. The poster's powerful imagery has fuelled discussion around its cultural roots and the Lord Murugan connection.

Christopher Nolan thrilled to launch The Odyssey in India, thanks fans. Watch Filmmaker Christopher Nolan opened The Odyssey in Mumbai with actors Tom Holland and Matt Damon and thanked the audience for its response. The launch marked Nolan's first film premiere in the city, placing India among the earliest stops on the global premiere tour.

The Odyssey gets A rating, Christopher Nolan's film passes without cuts in India Filmmaker Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey reportedly received an A certificate and cleared the censors without any cuts. The approval means Indian viewers are set to watch the same version as global audiences.

Lenin X review: Akhil wins praise, but weak writing splits viewers Actor Akhil Akkineni's Lenin opened to mixed reactions on X, with viewers split over its rural action drama and Mahabharata parallels. The response highlighted his performance but questioned the writing, romance and song placement.

Aryan Khan mobbed outside Raghav Juyal's birthday party, fan falls amid chaos Aryan Khan was mobbed by fans and paparazzi while leaving Raghav Juyal's birthday party in Mumbai. The viral video drew criticism online after a fan fell amid the pushing and shoving.

The Odyssey India premiere schedule: Nolan, Tom Holland to meet fans, media, stars Christopher Nolan and Tom Holland arrived in Mumbai on Friday ahead of The Odyssey's India premiere, marking the filmmaker's first-ever official film premiere in the country. Here's a complete schedule of the premiere, press conference and the film's worldwide release.

Viral video: Nora Fatehi breaks down after Morocco's World Cup quarter-final exit Actor-performer Nora Fatehi got emotional after Morocco crashed out of the FIFA World Cup in the quarter-finals. A video of the actor wiping away tears in the stands is going viral.

Ali Fazal shares handwritten note to fans for giving Raakh character home in hearts Actor Ali Fazal shared a handwritten note thanking fans for embracing his Raakh character, SI Jayprakash Jatav. The actor said his heart was "full" as the series continues to enjoy a strong global response on Prime Video.

Ajay Devgn's Dhamaal 4 confirms franchise future with Dhamaal 5 tease The makers have announced Dhamaal 5 in the final moments of Ajay Devgn's Dhamaal 4. Filmmaker Farhad Samji will direct the next instalment, even as audience reactions remain mixed.

Prakash Raj gets bail in multiple voter ID case in Bengaluru court Actor Prakash Raj appeared before a Bengaluru court in the voter ID case and sought bail after a non-bailable warrant. The court recalled the warrant after his counsel said summons were never served and granted bail on a cash surety of Rs 4,000.

National award-winning Tamil director-cinematographer Chezhiyan dies at 57 National Award-winning cinematographer, director and writer R Chezhiyan died in Chennai on Friday after a prolonged illness. Colleagues, actors and fans remembered his realist cinema and lasting contribution to Tamil films.

Idhayam Murali review: Atharvaa's love story fails to confess its own feelingsRelease Date:Fri Jul 10, 2026 Idhayam Murali movie review: Atharvaa headlines Idhayam Murali as a man who cannot confess his love. The film lands its biggest laughs through cameos and comedy but struggles to deepen its relationships.

Rajpal Yadav gets 3-month jail in cheque bounce case; court calls conduct 'dubious' The Delhi High Court upheld actor Rajpal Yadav's conviction in seven cheque bounce cases and sentenced him to three months' simple imprisonment, with all sentences to run concurrently. The court noted that Yadav repeatedly failed to honour his commitments to settle the dispute despite multiple opportunities.

BTS label denies Swim copyright lawsuit, says song was created independently BigHit Music has rejected a US copyright lawsuit alleging BTS' single Swim copied an unreleased demo by three American songwriters. The dispute centres on claims the demo was circulated within the industry before the chart-topping track's release.

Dhamaal 4 review: You will find buried treasure before you find the comedyRelease Date:Fri Jul 10, 2026 Dhamaal 4 review: Ajay Devgn's film follows a familiar treasure hunt as more players join the chase. But how far can weak writing, bad AI visuals and dated humour take the franchise's comic spark?

Govinda enters Lock Upp with bullet in pocket, dares wife Ssunita to shoot him Actor Govinda made a surprise appearance on Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa and jokingly responded to wife Ssunita Ahuja's earlier remark about shooting him in the chest.

Lenin review: Akhil Akkineni's film confuses a good story with a good movieRelease Date:Fri Jul 10, 2026 Lenin movie review: Akhil Akkineni's Lenin sets up a rural mystery around a prison returnee in Srirampuram. Its ambition and strong moments are undercut by weak character groundwork and shock-driven twists.

Lock Upp: Shreya Kalra claims Kushal Tandon DMed her while dating Shivangi Joshi Lock Upp 2 contestant Shreya Kalra alleged that Kushal Tandon had messaged her while he was dating Shivangi Joshi. She claimed she later showed their chats to Shivangi to clear any misunderstanding.

general news.headline: ESP 1-1 BEL Live: Spain hit by shock with De Ketelaere equaliserSpain (ESP) vs Belgium (BEL) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: (ESP 1-1 BEL) Spain dominated early and deservedly led through Fabian Ruiz, but Belgium hit back before the break as Charles De Ketelaere headed home to leave the quarter-final finely poised.

I've left instructions: Trump says bomb Iran if I'm assassinatedUS President Donald Trump said he has left instructions to launch an unprecedented military strike on Iran if Tehran succeeds in assassinating him, claiming he has long been Iran's "number one target."

Sinner demolishes Djokovic at Wimbledon semis in ruthless revengeJannik Sinner avenged his Australian Open semi-final defeat to Novak Djokovic with a commanding straight-sets win at Wimbledon, beating the Serbian legend 6-4, 6-4, 6-4 to reach his second successive final at the All England Club.

Chinese dam near India border a ticking geological bomb, finds Beijing-backed studyChinese geologists have raised red flags over the world's biggest hydropower project that China is building near the Indian border. A government-backed study has warned that the $137-billion Medog dam on the Brahmaputra is being built in one of the most quake-prone regions of the Himalayas.
The ethanol statistics that cannot stand upThe writer demolishes four claims of ethanol evangelists and explains why the sum of the scam is bigger than the parts.

H1-B faces fresh heat as Indian-origin CEO-led Xbox cuts jobsWith intensifying scrutiny of the H-1B visa programme, Microsoft's latest Xbox layoffs have handed anti-immigrants a fresh rallying point. Several commentators, including a Republican lawmaker, seized on the job cuts to attack the visa, claiming Indian-origin Asha Sharma-led Xbox was replacing American workers with cheaper foreign labour.

Zverev crushes Arthur Fery's Wimbledon dream to storm into maiden finalFresh off his maiden French Open triumph, Alexander Zverev continued his red-hot form by ending Arthur Fery's dream Wimbledon run to book a first final at the All England Club.

Rain did in 48 hours what policy failed to do in years for Delhi's airDelhi recorded an AQI of 48 on July 9, its first good air day since September 2023, after heavy monsoon rain scrubbed pollutants from the sky. Here is the science of how rain cleans the air, why a drizzle cannot, and why not a single day of 2026 has met the WHO safe limit.

When stoppage of Indus waters canal spooked Pakistan; Nehru called act inhumanNearly eight decades before the PM Narendra Modi-led government suspended the 1960s Indus Waters Treaty, the government of East Punjab in 1948 stopped the headworks at the Upper Bari Doab Canal, part of the Indus system. The crisis forced the architect of Pakistan, Muhammad Ali Jinnah, to negotiate with India.

Twist in Jaipur woman's murder, daughter now accused of killing father tooAyushi Sharma's maternal uncle has sought an investigation into her father Vijay Sharma's death. The move adds a fresh allegation to the Jaipur case, where police have already arrested seven accused.

Amaira was afraid of heights. Yet...: Mother's bullying charge in Jaipur school suicideOn Wednesday, Amaira's parents released CCTV footage and alleged their daughter was bullied and ignored in class before her fatal fall.

Mr judicial servant, I order you…: Man flings papers, abuses inside SC courtroomA complainant was forcibly removed from the Supreme Court after he shouted abuses and threw documents in the courtroom during a hearing of his petition.

Sabrina Pasterski built a plane at 12, rejected NASA, now called the next EinsteinSabrina Gonzalez Pasterski built and flew an aircraft as a teenager, graduated from MIT with a perfect GPA, had her research cited by Stephen Hawking, and turned down offers from NASA, Blue Origin and a reported $1.1 million academic position to pursue some of physics' biggest unanswered questions.

Trump says Iran sought talks, US agreed, but warns Tehran the ceasefire is overUS President Donald Trump said Iran has sought to continue talks with Washington but asserted that any ceasefire between the two countries has ended, making the remarks in a post on his Truth Social platform.

Heavy rain to continue across North and East India; Delhi, UP on alertMonsoon will continue to cast an influence over much of the country, but changing climate patterns could soon begin reshaping the course of the season.

Video: Vijay breaks down as he hands job letters to families of stampede victimsVijay broke down in tears as he consoled the grieving families of the Karur stampede victims while handing over government job appointment letters to them during his first visit to Karur after the tragedy.

Christopher Nolan thrilled to launch The Odyssey in India, thanks fans. WatchFilmmaker Christopher Nolan opened The Odyssey in Mumbai with actors Tom Holland and Matt Damon and thanked the audience for its response. The launch marked Nolan's first film premiere in the city, placing India among the earliest stops on the global premiere tour.

Hyderabad couple goes missing on Switzerland holiday, untraceable for 20 daysA Hyderabad couple lost contact with their family during a holiday in Switzerland, prompting a police investigation. With both phones switched off for over 20 days, officials are checking travel records and pursuing leads.

They might kill me: Hasina says will return to Bangladesh, surrender in DecemberFormer Bangladesh Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina said she and her Awami League colleagues would return to their home country in December and surrender to the authorities. Declaring that "they might kill me", Hasina said she would return from India.

Shraddha Walkar murder accused to write MA exam, court cancels July 20 hearingCourt cancelled proceedings in the Shraddha Walkar murder case on July 20 after accused Aaftab Poonawala said he would be appearing for his final MA Sociology exam in Tihar Jail,

Amarnath ice lingam melts just 5 days into the 57-day Yatra. Who's to blame?The Amarnath Yatra, a year after the Pahalgam terror attack, which hit tourism and pilgrimage, has drawn record pilgrims. But the sacred ice lingam in the cave shrine has shrunk dramatically in just five days. Over 50 days of the pilgrimage are still to go. Is climate change alone to blame, or is there more to the story?

Why isn't E20 cheaper? Govt clarifies price, mileage and engine concernsAmid growing concerns over E20 petrol's impact on older vehicles, the Centre has issued a detailed clarification addressing questions on mileage, engine safety, pricing, ethanol blending and the absence of pure petrol, while defending the nationwide fuel policy.

Kerala student in Uzbekistan killed by classmate; family alleges conversion bidA 21-year-old medical student from Kerala was allegedly killed by her classmate in Uzbekistan after an argument. Her family has sought a probe by local police, alleging a brutal assault and pressure to convert.

sports.headline: Sarvesh Kushare's Diamond League debut sees him snatch podium in men's high jump Sarvesh Kushare announced himself on the Diamond League stage with a historic podium finish in Monaco, clearing 2.26m to finish third in the men's high jump. The Indian national record holder became only the fourth Indian athlete to finish in the top three at a Diamond League meet.

Djokovic promises Wimbledon return after semi-final battering vs Sinner Novak Djokovic has vowed to return to Wimbledon next year despite suffering a crushing straight-sets defeat to Jannik Sinner in the semi-finals. The 39-year-old said he wants "at least one more" shot at the All England Club.

Sinner demolishes Djokovic at Wimbledon semis in ruthless revenge Jannik Sinner avenged his Australian Open semi-final defeat to Novak Djokovic with a commanding straight-sets win at Wimbledon, beating the Serbian legend 6-4, 6-4, 6-4 to reach his second successive final at the All England Club.

Lord's Test Day 1: India Women stutter to 285, but strike late to keep hosts at bay India Women were bowled out for 285 despite fifties from Smriti Mandhana, Harmanpreet Kaur and Deepti Sharma, but struck late through Kranti Goud to leave England at 21 for 1 at stumps on an evenly poised opening day at Lord's.

ESP 1-1 BEL Live: Ruiz helps Spain take lead, Belgium yet to make their move Spain (ESP) vs Belgium (BEL) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: (ESP 1-1 BEL) Spain dominated early and deservedly led through Fabian Ruiz, but Belgium hit back before the break as Charles De Ketelaere headed home to leave the quarter-final finely poised.

Cristiano Ronaldo's future, age and more: What new Portugal coach Jorge Jesus said New Portugal coach Jorge Jesus addressed Cristiano Ronaldo's international future during his unveiling, insisting the veteran remains part of his plans despite retiring from World Cup football and revealing he will hold talks before making a decision.

Zverev crushes Arthur Fery's Wimbledon dream to storm into maiden final Fresh off his maiden French Open triumph, Alexander Zverev continued his red-hot form by ending Arthur Fery's dream Wimbledon run to book a first final at the All England Club.

India A set for landmark tour of Nepal for three-match T20I series: Exclusive India A will tour Nepal for a landmark three-match T20I series on December 9, 11 and 13, India Today has learnt. The series marks a major boost for Nepal cricket, offering the hosts a chance to test themselves against India's emerging stars.
FIFA World Cup 2026 | Why India is missing As smaller nations light up the World Cup, Indian football languishes in neglect and indecision

Highlights: Sinner beats Djokovic in straight sets, sets up final vs Zverev Wimbledon semifinals Highlights: Novak Djokovic's run at Wimbledon gets cut short by World No.1 Jannik Sinner. The Italian beats Djokovic in straight sets (6-4,6-4, 6-4), booking a place in the final against Zlexander Zverev, who earlier beat British wildcard Arthur Fery in straight games.

Shree Charani rewarded for T20 World Cup heroics, earns maiden Test cap at Lord's ENGW vs INDW, Only Test Day 1: Shree Charani made her Test debut at the Lord's after her stupendous campaign in the Women's T20 World Cup where she took 14 wickets from five matches.

Only Women's Test, Day 1: Live Score and Updates from Lord'sEngland vs India, Only Women's Test, Day 1 Live Updates: England have won the toss and opted to field first at the Lord's. Alice Capsey and Mady Villiers are making their Test debuts for the hosts while Shree Charani got her maiden Test cap for India.

England chase No.1 T20I ranking after series win vs India, says Harry BrookEngland captain Harry Brook has targeted the No. 1 spot in the ICC men's T20I rankings after his side wrapped up the series against India, calling it "definitely a prize" worth chasing.

Coco Gauff hits back at haters after Wimbledon exit: Will tag them when I winCoco Gauff shrugged off the criticism following her Wimbledon heartbreak, saying hate from trolls and bettors will only fuel her motivation to bounce back stronger.

IPL in Australia after BBL's India announcement? Report makes sensational claimAn Indian Premier League (IPL) match could be played in Australia in the near future, with Cricket Australia (CA) reportedly inviting the BCCI to take the tournament overseas after securing a landmark Big Bash League (BBL) fixture in India.

Carlos Alcaraz resumes training, offers positive injury update ahead of US OpenAfter missing the French Open and Wimbledon, Carlos Alcaraz has raised hopes of a return before the US Open after sharing videos of himself back in training following a three-month injury layoff.

BCCI concerned over India's T20I slump, review planned after England seriesAfter back-to-back T20I series defeats against Ireland and England, the BCCI will review India's recent slump, with secretary Devajit Saikia admitting the board is "deeply concerned" by the team's performances.

Harmanpreet Kaur hopes Lord's Test can heal Women's T20 World Cup woundsENGW vs INDW: Harmanpreet Kaur hopes India's one-off Test against England at Lord's will help the team move on from the disappointment of the Women's T20 World Cup and rediscover its rhythm in the longest format.

Spain vs Belgium quarterfinal: Can Red Devils spring a surprise?Spain and Belgium are one win away from the 2026 FIFA World Cup semi-finals as they meet at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles on Friday. Spain have won all four of their matches without conceding a goal, while Belgium recovered from an uneven group-stage campaign with victories over Senegal and co-hosts United States to reach the last eight.

Shreyas Iyer backed amid mounting scrutiny after England T20I series debacleENG vs IND: India assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate has backed Shreyas Iyer despite the team's poor run in T20Is following series defeats to Ireland and England, urging critics to show patience with the new captain as India navigate a difficult phase.

England's Quansah served ban, US's Balogun didn't: FIFA decision sparks debateEngland defender Jarell Quansah will miss the World Cup quarter-final against Norway after FIFA handed him a two-match suspension for his red card against Mexico. Days earlier, United States striker Folarin Balogun escaped an immediate ban after being sent off for a similar challenge against Bosnia.

Thought Roger Federer was alone at Wimbledon? His cheeky reply says otherwiseRoger Federer's appearance in Wimbledon's Royal Box during the Championships sparked widespread discussion, with the image of the Swiss legend quickly going viral on social media. Former world No.1 Andy Roddick has now opened up about the moment.

Rubbish: Gautam Gambhir's oldest insult in England just got a new meaningA year apart, England has once again laid bare Gautam Gambhir's tactical shortcomings. Gambhir is undoubtedly one of the finest T20 coaches around, so why do his teams keep falling apart when the conditions become even slightly challenging?

Morocco coach eyes World Cup revenge after second straight defeat to FranceMorocco coach Mohamed Ouahbi vowed his side would return stronger after their World Cup campaign ended with a 2-0 defeat to France, saying the Atlas Lions would target revenge when the two teams meet again on the biggest stage.

PM Modi, Albanese lay down sports roadmap between India, Australia at iconic MCGPrime Minister Narendra Modi and Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese on Friday unveiled the India-Australia Sports Collaboration Roadmap at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG), as Australia backed India's bid to host the 2036 Olympic Games.

Big Bash League's opening match to be played in India, announces PM in AustraliaAustralia's premier domestic T20 competition, the Big Bash League (BBL), will make history later this year with its opening match set to be played in India for the first time.

India vs England: All you need to know about historic Lord's Women's TestIndia and England will make history by playing the first-ever women's Test at Lord's, with Harmanpreet Kaur's side eyeing only their second Test win over England despite the absence of injured opener Pratika Rawal.

Messi's World Cup message takes centre stage at Argentina Independence Day MassFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi's message of unity from Argentina's 2022 World Cup triumph was read aloud during the country's Independence Day Mass as the world champions prepare for another crucial knockout clash.

Haaland's pressure prank? England smile and call it mind gamesFIFA World Cup: Erling Haaland insists England have everything to lose in the World Cup quarter-finals, but the Three Lions believe the Norway striker is simply trying to get inside their heads.

England furious as Quansah red-card ban reignites FIFA consistency rowFIFA World Cup 2026: England have been left frustrated after Jarell Quansah received a two-match World Cup suspension, with former referees questioning why a similar challenge by Folarin Balogun resulted in a far lighter punishment.

Shreyas Iyer makes bizarre excuse after T20I series loss in BristolShreyas Iyer's attempt to explain India's T20I series defeat to England is unlikely to convince many, given how recently this team lifted the T20 World Cup. Speaking after England sealed an unassailable 3-0 lead with a dominant nine-wicket win in the fourth T20I at Bristol, Iyer said India were in a "transition phase" and that mistakes were inevitable.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
